summary: Iteration 78 testing completed. Price Premium Payable feature verified: Backend calculate_delivery_item_amounts() correctly calculates price_premium_payable = qty × (customer_selling_price - transfer_price) when customer_price > transfer_price. Frontend SettlementsTab shows 'Price Premium' column in table header and rows. Frontend BillingTab has Price Premium summary card. Excel export includes 'Price Premium Payable' column. Note: Existing data (pre-feature) doesn't have price_premium_payable field in database - this is expected behavior.

minor.issue: Existing settlements don't have price_premium_payable field in database (pre-feature data). Frontend handles this gracefully with || 0 fallback.
Existing deliveries don't have total_price_premium field in database (pre-feature data). Frontend handles this gracefully.

critical_code_review_comments: calculate_delivery_item_amounts() correctly implements price_premium_payable = qty × (customer_selling_price - transfer_price) when customer_price > transfer_price
Delivery creation correctly aggregates total_price_premium from item-level price_premium_payable
Settlement generation correctly calculates price_premium_payable per settlement from delivery items
SettlementsTab.jsx correctly displays 'Price Premium' column in table header (line 521) and rows (line 556-558)
SettlementsTab.jsx correctly aggregates price_premium in totals (line 107)
SettlementsTab.jsx Excel export includes 'Price Premium Payable' column (line 183)
BillingTab.jsx correctly displays Price Premium summary card (lines 396-406)
BillingTab.jsx correctly shows Price Premium column in settlement table (lines 464, 478-479)
Frontend uses || 0 fallback for missing price_premium_payable field - handles pre-feature data gracefully

success_rate.frontend: 100% - Price Premium column visible in Settlements tab, BillingTab has Price Premium card

context_for_next_testing_agent: Price Premium Payable feature is fully implemented. Backend calculates price_premium_payable at delivery item level, aggregates to delivery level (total_price_premium), and settlement level (price_premium_payable). Frontend displays Price Premium column in SettlementsTab table and BillingTab summary cards. Excel export includes Price Premium Payable column. Existing data (pre-feature) doesn't have these fields but frontend handles gracefully with || 0 fallback.

Backend_calculate_delivery_item_amounts.formula: price_premium_payable = qty × (customer_selling_price - transfer_price) when customer_price > transfer_price, else 0
